data.title: As a re-sync process, I can regenerate the full 3-artifact manifest by merging visual changes with preserved behavioral specs
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Takes the mapped spatial-layout.json from S2 and the original pre-revision 3-artifact manifest, merges visual changes with preserved behavioral specs, regenerates all 3 artifacts, and validates ID-linkage integrity.
**Why**: This is the core algorithm that makes Pull valuable — it replaces lossy image interpretation with structured, behavioral-preserving merge, giving the implementation agent the same format it already knows.
**Scope**:
- IN: Merge algorithm (visual from Figma overwrites, behavioral from original preserves), designer-added component flagging (tag: NEW, designer_added: true, behavioral_spec_status: INCOMPLETE), designer-removed cleanup with COMPLETE spec warning, wireframe.svg regeneration from updated spatial-layout.json, tokens.md visual section update with behavioral sections preserved, ID-linkage validation across all 3 artifacts, schema extension for designer_added and behavioral_spec_status fields
- OUT: URL/auth handling (S1), REST API retrieval (S2), re-sync orchestration (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the merge algorithm updates visual properties (positions, sizes, fills, strokes, text) from the Figma-derived spatial-layout.json while preserving all behavioral specifications (Interaction Behaviors, Responsive Rules, Accessibility Specification, State Definitions sections of tokens.md) from the original manifest
  ← Satisfies: "merging Figma's visual changes with the original behavioral specifications"
- When this story is complete, designer-added components are created with tag: NEW, designer_added: true, behavioral_spec_status: INCOMPLETE; the spatial-layout.json schema definition is updated to include these new optional fields
  ← Satisfies: "Designer-added components created as tag: NEW with designer_added: true"
- When this story is complete, designer-removed components are cleaned from all 3 artifacts; a WARNING is logged if the removed component had behavioral_spec_status: COMPLETE
  ← Satisfies: "removed components cleaned from all 3 artifacts with warning"
- When this story is complete, wireframe.svg is regenerated from the updated spatial-layout.json and tokens.md Design System Tokens Applied section is updated from Figma while behavioral sections are preserved
  ← Satisfies: "regenerates the complete 3-artifact design manifest"
- When this story is complete, ID-linkage is validated after merge: every component ID in spatial-layout.json exists in wireframe.svg and is referenced in tokens.md, with no orphaned references in any artifact
  ← Satisfies: "ID-linkage validated"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Merge needs test fixtures: designer-added components, removed components with COMPLETE specs, renamed/repositioned components
- [Reliability] Warn before deleting COMPLETE behavioral specs — this is irreversible data loss
- [Maintainability] Reuse design-wireframe ID-linkage validation logic where possible
- [Dependency] SVG regeneration requires a reusable SVG generation capability — verify whether design-wireframe has a factored function or if extraction is needed

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
